revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:Former Gō-sha in Gunma Prefecture
revision.text:
{{infobox religious building |name = Shidori Shrine |image = [[File:Shidori-jinja (Isesaki) haiden-1.JPG|270px]]
Haiden (Worship Hall) |location = [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], [[:en:Isesaki|Isesaki]], Higashi-Kaminomiya-cho 380 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|18|39.9|N|139|9|5.1|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shidori Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|4=simple|5=Takehazuchi|6=fr|lt=Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} |shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Minor)
[[:en:Ichinomiya|Ichinomiya]] (9th Rank of Kōzuke Province)
Former {{ill|Gōsha (Japan)|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Japan)|qq=}} |established = (Legendary) 3rd year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] |main_hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |other_names = Kaminomiya (Upper Shrine) |festival = April 16 |ceremonies = Ta-asobi (January 14) |map = {{location map|Japan Gunma|label=Shidori Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|36|18|39.9|N|139|9|5.1|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|18|39.9|N|139|9|5.1|E}}}} }} {{Coordinate list}} [[File:Shidori-jinja (Isesaki)Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Torii Gate]] '''Shidori Shrine''' (倭文神社, ''Shidori Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Isesaki|Isesaki]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is a recognized {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Minor), Ranked as the 9th shrine of [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]], and was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha (Japan)|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Japan)|qq=}} under the old shrine Ranking system. It is situated on the left bank of the [[:en:Tone River|Tone River]], while {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|火雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} (the 8th-Ranked shrine of Kōzuke Province, also called "Shimonomiya" or "Lower Shrine") is on the right bank. Due to this, Shidori Shrine is often referred to as '''Kaminomiya''' (Upper Shrine). == Enshrined Deities == '''Main deity''' * '''{{ill|Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|4=simple|5=Takehazuchi|6=fr|lt=Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (God of weaving and sericulture, also known as "Shidori no Kami") '''Additional Deities''' * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Toyouke-hime|ja|豊受姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}}, {{ill|Kukurihime|ja|菊理姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukurihime|qq=}} == History == === Founding === [[File:Shidori-jinja (Isesaki) hengaku.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Plaque with Shrine Name]] According to shrine tradition, Shidori Shrine was founded in the 3rd year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], though details remain unclear. The term "Shidori" is derived from "Shizuori" (倭文, meaning woven fabric). The deity {{ill|Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|qq=}} was the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Shidori-be|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori-be|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, a weaving guild. This suggests that a community of Shidori-be people settled in the area and established the shrine. The shrine’s name is thought to have influenced the local place name "Kaminomiya" (Upper Shrine). It faces {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|火雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} (Shimonomiya, Lower Shrine) across the Tone River, though historical records indicate that the two were once connected before the river changed course in the medieval period''Gunma Prefecture Place Names,'' Sawa District, Tamamura Town, Shimonomiya Village. === Historical Records === The first mention of the shrine in historical documents is in the ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (859 CE), where it appears as "Ibumi Shrine" (委文神) and was granted the status of an official government shrine. That same year, it was elevated to the Rank of ''Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade'' (従五位下). In the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'' (927 CE), the shrine is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, confirming its importance in the [[:en:Nawa District, Gunma|Naba District]] of Kōzuke Province. In 1030, the shrine is recorded in the ''Jōgen 3 Exchange Records'' (上野国交替実録帳) as "Ibumi Myōjin Shrine" (委文明神社), holding the Rank of ''Senior Third Rank'' (正三位). The ''[[:en:Shintōshū|Jindōshū]]'' (compiled in the Nanboku-chō period) refers to the shrine as the 9th shrine of Kōzuke Province. === Edo Period and Beyond === During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the temple {{ill|Jigen-ji|ja|慈眼寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigen-ji|qq=}} became the shrine's caretaker, and in 1648, the Tokugawa Shogunate granted it an official landholding of 10 koku. In 1727, the shrine buildings were rebuilt but were later destroyed in 1866. During the Meiji Restoration, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha (Japan)|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Japan)|qq=}}. In 1925, it was recognized as a recipient of imperial offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). == Shrine Structures == The main hall (''honden'') is built in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style with a copper roof and was rebuilt in 1880. File:Shidori-jinja (Isesaki) honden.JPG|Main Hall File:Shidori-jinja (Isesaki) haiden-2.JPG|Worship Hall == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties of Isesaki City === * '''Important Cultural Property''' ** Shidori Shrine’s Red Seal Certificates – A set of nine ''shuinjō'' (朱印状) issued since 1648, designated in 1992. * '''Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property''' ** ''Ta-asobi'' (田遊び) – A traditional rice-planting ritual performed on January 14, designated in 2007. == Related Shrines == * {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|火雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} – Located in Tamamura Town, historically connected as "Shimonomiya" (Lower Shrine). == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shidori-jinja (Isesaki)}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as Amanoorihime) }} }} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{citation needed|ja|静神社|version=101958783|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Isesaki, Gunma]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Weaving and textiles in Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to textile deities]] [[Category:Mythological and legendary Japanese figures]] [[Category:Imported Shinto cults]] [[Category:Shizuri Shrines]] }} [[ja:倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)]] [[ja:静神社]] [[ja:那珂市]] [[sn:Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)]] [[ja:倭文神社 (韮崎市)]] [[ja:韮崎市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (富士宮市)]] [[ja:富士宮市]] [[ja:三島市]] [[ja:鍬戸神社]] [[ja:加佐登神社]] [[ja:倭文神社 (舞鶴市)]] [[ja:舞鶴市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (与謝野町)]] [[ja:与謝野町]] [[ja:倭文神社 (朝来市)]] [[ja:朝来市]] In 1925, it was recognized as a recipient of imperial offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). == Shrine Structures == The main hall (''honden'') is built in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style with a copper roof and was rebuilt in 1880. File:Shidori-jinja (Isesaki) honden.JPG|Main Hall File:Shidori-jinja (Isesaki) haiden-2.JPG|Worship Hall == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties of Isesaki City === * '''Important Cultural Property''' ** Shidori Shrine’s Red Seal Certificates – A set of nine ''shuinjō'' (朱印状) issued since 1648, designated in 1992. * '''Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property''' ** ''Ta-asobi'' (田遊び) – A traditional rice-planting ritual performed on January 14, designated in 2007. == Related Shrines == * {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|火雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} – Located in Tamamura Town, historically connected as "Shimonomiya" (Lower Shrine). == References == {{reflist}} {{infobox religious building |name = Shidori Shrine |image = [[File:Shidori-jinja (Isesaki) haiden-1.JPG|270px]]
Haiden (Worship Hall) |location = [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], [[:en:Isesaki|Isesaki]], Higashi-Kaminomiya-cho 380 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|18|39.9|N|139|9|5.1|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shidori Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|4=simple|5=Takehazuchi|6=fr|lt=Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} |shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Minor)
[[:en:Ichinomiya|Ichinomiya]] (9th Rank of Kōzuke Province)
Former {{ill|Gōsha (Japan)|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Japan)|qq=}} |established = (Legendary) 3rd year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] |main_hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |other_names = Kaminomiya (Upper Shrine) |festival = April 16 |ceremonies = Ta-asobi (January 14) |map = {{location map|Japan Gunma|label=Shidori Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|36|18|39.9|N|139|9|5.1|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|18|39.9|N|139|9|5.1|E}}}} }} {{Coordinate list}} [[File:Shidori-jinja (Isesaki)Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Torii Gate]] '''Shidori Shrine''' (倭文神社, ''Shidori Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Isesaki|Isesaki]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is a recognized {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Minor), Ranked as the 9th shrine of [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]], and was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha (Japan)|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Japan)|qq=}} under the old shrine Ranking system. It is situated on the left bank of the [[:en:Tone River|Tone River]], while {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|火雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} (the 8th-Ranked shrine of Kōzuke Province, also called "Shimonomiya" or "Lower Shrine") is on the right bank. Due to this, Shidori Shrine is often referred to as '''Kaminomiya''' (Upper Shrine). == Enshrined Deities == '''Main deity''' * '''{{ill|Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|4=simple|5=Takehazuchi|6=fr|lt=Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (God of weaving and sericulture, also known as "Shidori no Kami") '''Additional Deities''' * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Toyouke-hime|ja|豊受姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}}, {{ill|Kukurihime|ja|菊理姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukurihime|qq=}} == History == === Founding === [[File:Shidori-jinja (Isesaki) hengaku.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Plaque with Shrine Name]] According to shrine tradition, Shidori Shrine was founded in the 3rd year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], though details remain unclear. The term "Shidori" is derived from "Shizuori" (倭文, meaning woven fabric). The deity {{ill|Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|qq=}} was the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Shidori-be|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori-be|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, a weaving guild. This suggests that a community of Shidori-be people settled in the area and established the shrine. The shrine’s name is thought to have influenced the local place name "Kaminomiya" (Upper Shrine). It faces {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|火雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} (Shimonomiya, Lower Shrine) across the Tone River, though historical records indicate that the two were once connected before the river changed course in the medieval period''Gunma Prefecture Place Names,'' Sawa District, Tamamura Town, Shimonomiya Village. === Historical Records === The first mention of the shrine in historical documents is in the ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (859 CE), where it appears as "Ibumi Shrine" (委文神) and was granted the status of an official government shrine. That same year, it was elevated to the Rank of ''Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade'' (従五位下). In the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'' (927 CE), the shrine is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, confirming its importance in the [[:en:Nawa District, Gunma|Naba District]] of Kōzuke Province. In 1030, the shrine is recorded in the ''Jōgen 3 Exchange Records'' (上野国交替実録帳) as "Ibumi Myōjin Shrine" (委文明神社), holding the Rank of ''Senior Third Rank'' (正三位). The ''[[:en:Shintōshū|Jindōshū]]'' (compiled in the Nanboku-chō period) refers to the shrine as the 9th shrine of Kōzuke Province. === Edo Period and Beyond === During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the temple {{ill|Jigen-ji|ja|慈眼寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigen-ji|qq=}} became the shrine's caretaker, and in 1648, the Tokugawa Shogunate granted it an official landholding of 10 koku. In 1727, the shrine buildings were rebuilt but were later destroyed in 1866. During the Meiji Restoration, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha (Japan)|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Japan)|qq=}}. In 1925, it was recognized as a recipient of imperial offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). == Shrine Structures == The main hall (''honden'') is built in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style with a copper roof and was rebuilt in 1880. File:Shidori-jinja (Isesaki) honden.JPG|Main Hall File:Shidori-jinja (Isesaki) haiden-2.JPG|Worship Hall == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties of Isesaki City === * '''Important Cultural Property''' ** Shidori Shrine’s Red Seal Certificates – A set of nine ''shuinjō'' (朱印状) issued since 1648, designated in 1992. * '''Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property''' ** ''Ta-asobi'' (田遊び) – A traditional rice-planting ritual performed on January 14, designated in 2007. == Related Shrines == * {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|火雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} – Located in Tamamura Town, historically connected as "Shimonomiya" (Lower Shrine). == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shidori-jinja (Isesaki)}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as Amanoorihime) }} }} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{citation Due to this, Shidori Shrine is often referred to as '''Kaminomiya''' (Upper Shrine). == Enshrined Deities == '''Main deity''' * '''{{ill|Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|4=simple|5=Takehazuchi|6=fr|lt=Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (God of weaving and sericulture, also known as "Shidori no Kami") '''Additional Deities''' * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Toyouke-hime|ja|豊受姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}}, {{ill|Kukurihime|ja|菊理姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukurihime|qq=}} == History == === Founding === [[File:Shidori-jinja (Isesaki) hengaku.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Plaque with Shrine Name]] According to shrine tradition, Shidori Shrine was founded in the 3rd year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], though details remain unclear. The term "Shidori" is derived from "Shizuori" (倭文, meaning woven fabric). The deity {{ill|Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|qq=}} was the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Shidori-be|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori-be|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, a weaving guild. This suggests that a community of Shidori-be people settled in the area and established the shrine. The shrine’s name is thought to have influenced the local place name "Kaminomiya" (Upper Shrine). It faces {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|火雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} (Shimonomiya, Lower Shrine) across the Tone River, though historical records indicate that the two were once connected before the river changed course in the medieval period''Gunma Prefecture Place Names,'' Sawa District, Tamamura Town, Shimonomiya Village. === Historical Records === The first mention of the shrine in historical documents is in the ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (859 CE), where it appears as "Ibumi Shrine" (委文神) and was granted the status of an official government shrine. That same year, it was elevated to the Rank of ''Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade'' (従五位下). In the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'' (927 CE), the shrine is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, confirming its importance in the [[:en:Nawa District, Gunma|Naba District]] of Kōzuke Province. In 1030, the shrine is recorded in the ''Jōgen 3 Exchange Records'' (上野国交替実録帳) as "Ibumi Myōjin Shrine" (委文明神社), holding the Rank of ''Senior Third Rank'' (正三位). The ''[[:en:Shintōshū|Jindōshū]]'' (compiled in the Nanboku-chō period) refers to the shrine as the 9th shrine of Kōzuke Province. === Edo Period and Beyond === During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the temple {{ill|Jigen-ji|ja|慈眼寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigen-ji|qq=}} became the shrine's caretaker, and in 1648, the Tokugawa Shogunate granted it an official landholding of 10 koku. In 1727, the shrine buildings were rebuilt but were later destroyed in 1866. During the Meiji Restoration, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha (Japan)|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Japan)|qq=}}. In 1925, it was recognized as a recipient of imperial offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). == Shrine Structures == The main hall (''honden'') is built in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style with a copper roof and was rebuilt in 1880. File:Shidori-jinja (Isesaki) honden.JPG|Main Hall File:Shidori-jinja (Isesaki) haiden-2.JPG|Worship Hall == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties of Isesaki City === * '''Important Cultural Property''' ** Shidori Shrine’s Red Seal Certificates – A set of nine ''shuinjō'' (朱印状) issued since 1648, designated in 1992. * '''Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property''' ** ''Ta-asobi'' (田遊び) – A traditional rice-planting ritual performed on January 14, designated in 2007. == Related Shrines == * {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|火雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} – Located in Tamamura Town, historically connected as "Shimonomiya" (Lower Shrine). == References == {{reflist}} [[:en:Ichinomiya|Ichinomiya]] (9th Rank of Kōzuke Province)
Former {{ill|Gōsha (Japan)|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Japan)|qq=}} |established = (Legendary) 3rd year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] |main_hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |other_names = Kaminomiya (Upper Shrine) |festival = April 16 |ceremonies = Ta-asobi (January 14) |map = {{location map|Japan Gunma|label=Shidori Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|36|18|39.9|N|139|9|5.1|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|18|39.9|N|139|9|5.1|E}}}} }} {{Coordinate list}} [[File:Shidori-jinja (Isesaki)Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Torii Gate]] '''Shidori Shrine''' (倭文神社, ''Shidori Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Isesaki|Isesaki]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is a recognized {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Minor), Ranked as the 9th shrine of [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]], and was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha (Japan)|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Japan)|qq=}} under the old shrine Ranking system. It is situated on the left bank of the [[:en:Tone River|Tone River]], while {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|火雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} (the 8th-Ranked shrine of Kōzuke Province, also called "Shimonomiya" or "Lower Shrine") is on the right bank. Haiden (Worship Hall) |location = [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], [[:en:Isesaki|Isesaki]], Higashi-Kaminomiya-cho 380 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|18|39.9|N|139|9|5.1|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shidori Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|4=simple|5=Takehazuchi|6=fr|lt=Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} |shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (Minor)
[[:en:Ichinomiya|Ichinomiya]] (9th Rank of Kōzuke Province)
Former {{ill|Gōsha (Japan)|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Japan)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |established = (Legendary) 3rd year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] |main_hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |other_names = Kaminomiya (Upper Shrine) |festival = April 16 |ceremonies = Ta-asobi (January 14) |map = {{location map|Japan Gunma|label=Shidori Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|36|18|39.9|N|139|9|5.1|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|18|39.9|N|139|9|5.1|E}}}} }} {{Coordinate list}} [[File:Shidori-jinja (Isesaki)Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Torii Gate]] '''Shidori Shrine''' (倭文神社, ''Shidori Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Isesaki|Isesaki]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is a recognized {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (Minor), Ranked as the 9th shrine of [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]], and was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha (Japan)|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Japan)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the old shrine Ranking system. It is situated on the left bank of the [[:en:Tone River|Tone River]], while {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|火雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} (the 8th-Ranked shrine of Kōzuke Province, also called "Shimonomiya" or "Lower Shrine") is on the right bank. Due to this, Shidori Shrine is often referred to as '''Kaminomiya''' (Upper Shrine). == Enshrined Deities == '''Main deity''' * '''{{ill|Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|4=simple|5=Takehazuchi|6=fr|lt=Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (God of weaving and sericulture, also known as "Shidori no Kami") '''Additional Deities''' * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Toyouke-hime|ja|豊受姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, {{ill|Kukurihime|ja|菊理姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukurihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 菊理媛神}} == History == === Founding === [[File:Shidori-jinja (Isesaki) hengaku.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Plaque with Shrine Name]] According to shrine tradition, Shidori Shrine was founded in the 3rd year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], though details remain unclear. The term "Shidori" is derived from "Shizuori" (倭文, meaning woven fabric). The deity {{ill|Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Shidori-be|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori-be|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, a weaving guild. This suggests that a community of Shidori-be people settled in the area and established the shrine. The shrine’s name is thought to have influenced the local place name "Kaminomiya" (Upper Shrine). It faces {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|火雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} (Shimonomiya, Lower Shrine) across the Tone River, though historical records indicate that the two were once connected before the river changed course in the medieval period''Gunma Prefecture Place Names,'' Sawa District, Tamamura Town, Shimonomiya Village. === Historical Records === The first mention of the shrine in historical documents is in the ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (859 CE), where it appears as "Ibumi Shrine" (委文神) and was granted the status of an official government shrine. That same year, it was elevated to the Rank of ''Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade'' (従五位下). In the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'' (927 CE), the shrine is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, confirming its importance in the [[:en:Nawa District, Gunma|Naba District]] of Kōzuke Province. In 1030, the shrine is recorded in the ''Jōgen 3 Exchange Records'' (上野国交替実録帳) as "Ibumi Myōjin Shrine" (委文明神社), holding the Rank of ''Senior Third Rank'' (正三位). The ''[[:en:Shintōshū|Jindōshū]]'' (compiled in the Nanboku-chō period) refers to the shrine as the 9th shrine of Kōzuke Province. === Edo Period and Beyond === During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the temple {{ill|Jigen-ji|ja|慈眼寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigen-ji|qq=}} became the shrine's caretaker, and in 1648, the Tokugawa Shogunate granted it an official landholding of 10 koku. In 1727, the shrine buildings were rebuilt but were later destroyed in 1866. During the Meiji Restoration, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha (Japan)|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Japan)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. In 1925, it was recognized as a recipient of imperial offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). == Shrine Structures == The main hall (''honden'') is built in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style with a copper roof and was rebuilt in 1880. File:Shidori-jinja (Isesaki) honden.JPG|Main Hall File:Shidori-jinja (Isesaki) haiden-2.JPG|Worship Hall == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties of Isesaki City === * '''Important Cultural Property''' ** Shidori Shrine’s Red Seal Certificates – A set of nine ''shuinjō'' (朱印状) issued since 1648, designated in 1992. * '''Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property''' ** ''Ta-asobi'' (田遊び) – A traditional rice-planting ritual performed on January 14, designated in 2007. == Related Shrines == * {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|火雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} – Located in Tamamura Town, historically connected as "Shimonomiya" (Lower Shrine). == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shidori-jinja (Isesaki)}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as Amanoorihime) }} }} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{citation needed|ja|静神社|version=101958783|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] deities]] [[Category:Mythological and legendary Japanese figures]] [[Category:Imported Shinto cults]] [[Category:Shizuri Shrines]] }} [[ja:倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)]] [[ja:静神社]] [[ja:那珂市]] [[sn:Shidori Shrine In 1925, it was recognized as a recipient of imperial offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). == Shrine Structures == The main hall (''honden'') is built in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style with a copper roof and was rebuilt in 1880. Cultural Property''' ** Shidori Shrine’s Red Seal Certificates – A set of nine ''shuinjō'' (朱印状) issued since 1648, designated in 1992. * '''Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property''' ** ''Ta-asobi'' (田遊び) – A traditional rice-planting ritual performed on January 14, designated in 2007. == Related Shrines == * {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|火雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} – Located in Tamamura Town, historically connected as "Shimonomiya" (Lower Shrine). == References == {{reflist}} (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as Amanoorihime) }} }} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{citation revision.text:
{{infobox religious building |name = Shidori Shrine |image = [[File:Shidori-jinja (Isesaki) haiden-1.JPG|270px]]
Haiden (Worship Hall) |location = [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], [[:en:Isesaki|Isesaki]], Higashi-Kaminomiya-cho 380 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|18|39.9|N|139|9|5.1|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shidori Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|4=simple|5=Takehazuchi|6=fr|lt=Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} |shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (Minor)
[[:en:Ichinomiya|Ichinomiya]] (9th Rank of Kōzuke Province)
Former {{ill|Gōsha (Japan)|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Japan)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |established = (Legendary) 3rd year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] |main_hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |other_names = Kaminomiya (Upper Shrine) |festival = April 16 |ceremonies = Ta-asobi (January 14) |map = {{location map|Japan Gunma|label=Shidori Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|36|18|39.9|N|139|9|5.1|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|18|39.9|N|139|9|5.1|E}}}} }} {{Coordinate list}} [[File:Shidori-jinja (Isesaki)Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Torii Gate]] '''Shidori Shrine''' (倭文神社, ''Shidori Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Isesaki|Isesaki]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is a recognized {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (Minor), Ranked as the 9th shrine of [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]], and was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha (Japan)|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Japan)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the old shrine Ranking system. It is situated on the left bank of the [[:en:Tone River|Tone River]], while {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|火雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} (the 8th-Ranked shrine of Kōzuke Province, also called "Shimonomiya" or "Lower Shrine") is on the right bank. Due to this, Shidori Shrine is often referred to as '''Kaminomiya''' (Upper Shrine). == Enshrined Deities == '''Main deity''' * '''{{ill|Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|4=simple|5=Takehazuchi|6=fr|lt=Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (God of weaving and sericulture, also known as "Shidori no Kami") '''Additional Deities''' * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Toyouke-hime|ja|豊受姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, {{ill|Kukurihime|ja|菊理姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukurihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 菊理媛神}} == History == === Founding === [[File:Shidori-jinja (Isesaki) hengaku.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Plaque with Shrine Name]] According to shrine tradition, Shidori Shrine was founded in the 3rd year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], though details remain unclear. The term "Shidori" is derived from "Shizuori" (倭文, meaning woven fabric). The deity {{ill|Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Shidori-be|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori-be|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, a weaving guild. This suggests that a community of Shidori-be people settled in the area and established the shrine. The shrine’s name is thought to have influenced the local place name "Kaminomiya" (Upper Shrine). It faces {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|火雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} (Shimonomiya, Lower Shrine) across the Tone River, though historical records indicate that the two were once connected before the river changed course in the medieval period''Gunma Prefecture Place Names,'' Sawa District, Tamamura Town, Shimonomiya Village. === Historical Records === The first mention of the shrine in historical documents is in the ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (859 CE), where it appears as "Ibumi Shrine" (委文神) and was granted the status of an official government shrine. That same year, it was elevated to the Rank of ''Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade'' (従五位下). In the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'' (927 CE), the shrine is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, confirming its importance in the [[:en:Nawa District, Gunma|Naba District]] of Kōzuke Province. In 1030, the shrine is recorded in the ''Jōgen 3 Exchange Records'' (上野国交替実録帳) as "Ibumi Myōjin Shrine" (委文明神社), holding the Rank of ''Senior Third Rank'' (正三位). The ''[[:en:Shintōshū|Jindōshū]]'' (compiled in the Nanboku-chō period) refers to the shrine as the 9th shrine of Kōzuke Province. === Edo Period and Beyond === During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the temple {{ill|Jigen-ji|ja|慈眼寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigen-ji|qq=}} became the shrine's caretaker, and in 1648, the Tokugawa Shogunate granted it an official landholding of 10 koku. In 1727, the shrine buildings were rebuilt but were later destroyed in 1866. During the Meiji Restoration, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha (Japan)|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Japan)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. In 1925, it was recognized as a recipient of imperial offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). == Shrine Structures == The main hall (''honden'') is built in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style with a copper roof and was rebuilt in 1880. File:Shidori-jinja (Isesaki) honden.JPG|Main Hall File:Shidori-jinja (Isesaki) haiden-2.JPG|Worship Hall == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties of Isesaki City === * '''Important Cultural Property''' ** Shidori Shrine’s Red Seal Certificates – A set of nine ''shuinjō'' (朱印状) issued since 1648, designated in 1992. * '''Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property''' ** ''Ta-asobi'' (田遊び) – A traditional rice-planting ritual performed on January 14, designated in 2007. == Related Shrines == * {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|火雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} – Located in Tamamura Town, historically connected as "Shimonomiya" (Lower Shrine). == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shidori-jinja (Isesaki)}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as Amanoorihime) }} }} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{citation needed|ja|静神社|version=101958783|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Isesaki, Gunma]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Weaving and textiles in Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to textile deities]] [[Category:Shizuri Shrines]] }} [[ja:倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)]] [[ja:静神社]] [[ja:那珂市]] [[sn:Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)]] [[ja:倭文神社 (韮崎市)]] [[ja:韮崎市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (富士宮市)]] [[ja:富士宮市]] [[ja:三島市]] [[ja:鍬戸神社]] Michizane命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Toyouke-hime|ja|豊受姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, {{ill|Kukurihime|ja|菊理姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukurihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 菊理媛神}} == History == === Founding === [[File:Shidori-jinja (Isesaki) hengaku.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Plaque with Shrine Name]] According to shrine tradition, Shidori Shrine was founded in the 3rd year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], though details remain unclear. The term "Shidori" is derived from "Shizuori" (倭文, meaning woven fabric). The deity {{ill|Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Shidori-be|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori-be|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, a weaving guild. This suggests that a community of Shidori-be people settled in the area and established the shrine. The shrine’s name is thought to have influenced the local place name "Kaminomiya" (Upper Shrine). It faces {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|火雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} (Shimonomiya, Lower Shrine) across the Tone River, though historical records indicate that the two were once connected before the river changed course in the medieval period''Gunma Prefecture Place Names,'' Sawa District, Tamamura Town, Shimonomiya Village. === Historical Records === The first mention of the shrine in historical documents is in the ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (859 CE), where it appears as "Ibumi Shrine" (委文神) and was granted the status of an official government shrine. That same year, it was elevated to the Rank of ''Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade'' (従五位下). In the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'' (927 CE), the shrine is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, confirming its importance in the [[:en:Nawa District, Gunma|Naba District]] of Kōzuke Province. In 1030, the shrine is recorded in the ''Jōgen 3 Exchange Records'' (上野国交替実録帳) as "Ibumi Myōjin Shrine" (委文明神社), holding the Rank of ''Senior Third Rank'' (正三位). The ''[[:en:Shintōshū|Jindōshū]]'' (compiled in the Nanboku-chō period) refers to the shrine as the 9th shrine of Kōzuke Province. === Edo Period and Beyond === During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the temple {{ill|Jigen-ji|ja|慈眼寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigen-ji|qq=}} became the shrine's caretaker, and in 1648, the Tokugawa Shogunate granted it an official landholding of 10 koku. In 1727, the shrine buildings were rebuilt but were later destroyed in 1866. During the Meiji Restoration, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha (Japan)|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Japan)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. In 1925, it was recognized as a recipient of imperial offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). == Shrine Structures == The main hall (''honden'') is built in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style with a copper roof and was rebuilt in 1880. File:Shidori-jinja (Isesaki) honden.JPG|Main Hall File:Shidori-jinja (Isesaki) haiden-2.JPG|Worship Hall == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties of Isesaki City === * '''Important Cultural Property''' ** Shidori Shrine’s Red Seal Certificates – A set of nine ''shuinjō'' (朱印状) issued since 1648, designated in 1992. * '''Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property''' ** ''Ta-asobi'' (田遊び) – A traditional rice-planting ritual performed on January 14, designated in 2007. == Related Shrines == * {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|火雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} – Located in Tamamura Town, historically connected as "Shimonomiya" (Lower Shrine). == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shidori-jinja (Isesaki)}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as Amanoorihime) }} }} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{citation {{infobox religious building |name = Shidori Shrine |image = [[File:Shidori-jinja (Isesaki) haiden-1.JPG|270px]]
Haiden (Worship Hall) |location = [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], [[:en:Isesaki|Isesaki]], Higashi-Kaminomiya-cho 380 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|18|39.9|N|139|9|5.1|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shidori Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|4=simple|5=Takehazuchi|6=fr|lt=Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} |shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (Minor)
[[:en:Ichinomiya|Ichinomiya]] (9th Rank of Kōzuke Province)
Former {{ill|Gōsha (Japan)|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Japan)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |established = (Legendary) 3rd year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] |main_hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |other_names = Kaminomiya (Upper Shrine) |festival = April 16 |ceremonies = Ta-asobi (January 14) |map = {{location map|Japan Gunma|label=Shidori Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|36|18|39.9|N|139|9|5.1|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|18|39.9|N|139|9|5.1|E}}}} }} {{Coordinate list}} [[File:Shidori-jinja (Isesaki)Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Torii Gate]] '''Shidori Shrine''' (倭文神社, ''Shidori Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Isesaki|Isesaki]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is a recognized {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (Minor), Ranked as the 9th shrine of [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]], and was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha (Japan)|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Japan)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the old shrine Ranking system. It is situated on the left bank of the [[:en:Tone River|Tone River]], while {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|火雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} (the 8th-Ranked shrine of Kōzuke Province, also called "Shimonomiya" or "Lower Shrine") is on the right bank. Due to this, Shidori Shrine is often referred to as '''Kaminomiya''' (Upper Shrine). == Enshrined Deities == '''Main deity''' * '''{{ill|Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|4=simple|5=Takehazuchi|6=fr|lt=Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (God of weaving and sericulture, also known as "Shidori no Kami") '''Additional Deities''' * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * hengaku.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Plaque with Shrine Name]] According to shrine tradition, Shidori Shrine was founded in the 3rd year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], though details remain unclear. The term "Shidori" is derived from "Shizuori" (倭文, meaning woven fabric). The deity {{ill|Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Shidori-be|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori-be|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, a weaving guild. It faces {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|火雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} (Shimonomiya, Lower Shrine) across the Tone River, though historical records indicate that the two were once connected before the river changed course in the medieval period''Gunma Prefecture Place Names,'' Sawa District, Tamamura Town, Shimonomiya Village. === Historical Records === The first mention of the shrine in historical documents is in the ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (859 CE), where it appears as "Ibumi Shrine" (委文神) and was granted the status of an official government shrine. That same year, it was elevated to the Rank of ''Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade'' (従五位下). In the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'' (927 CE), the shrine is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, confirming its importance in the [[:en:Nawa District, Gunma|Naba District]] of Kōzuke Province. In 1727, the shrine buildings were rebuilt but were later destroyed in 1866. During the Meiji Restoration, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha (Japan)|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Japan)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. {{infobox religious building |name = Shidori Shrine |image = [[File:Shidori-jinja (Isesaki) haiden-1.JPG|270px]]
Haiden (Worship Hall) |location = [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], [[:en:Isesaki|Isesaki]], Higashi-Kaminomiya-cho 380 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|18|39.9|N|139|9|5.1|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shidori Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|4=simple|5=Takehazuchi|6=fr|lt=Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} |shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (Minor)
[[:en:Ichinomiya|Ichinomiya]] (9th Rank of Kōzuke Province)
Former {{ill|Gōsha (Japan)|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Japan)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |established = (Legendary) 3rd year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] |main_hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |other_names = Kaminomiya (Upper Shrine) |festival = April 16 |ceremonies = Ta-asobi (January 14) |map = {{location map|Japan Gunma|label=Shidori Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|36|18|39.9|N|139|9|5.1|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|18|39.9|N|139|9|5.1|E}}}} }} {{Coordinate list}} [[File:Shidori-jinja (Isesaki)Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Torii Gate]] '''Shidori Shrine''' (倭文神社, ''Shidori Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Isesaki|Isesaki]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is a recognized {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (Minor), Ranked as the 9th shrine of [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]], and was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha (Japan)|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Japan)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the old shrine Ranking system. It is situated on the left bank of the [[:en:Tone River|Tone River]], while {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|火雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} (the 8th-Ranked shrine of Kōzuke Province, also called "Shimonomiya" or "Lower Shrine") is on the right bank. Due to this, Shidori Shrine is often referred to as '''Kaminomiya''' (Upper Shrine). == Enshrined Deities == '''Main deity''' * '''{{ill|Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|4=simple|5=Takehazuchi|6=fr|lt=Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (God of weaving and sericulture, also known as "Shidori no Kami") '''Additional Deities''' * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Toyouke-hime|ja|豊受姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, {{ill|Kukurihime|ja|菊理姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukurihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 菊理媛神}} == History == === Founding === [[File:Shidori-jinja (Isesaki) hengaku.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Plaque with Shrine Name]] According to shrine tradition, Shidori Shrine was founded in the 3rd year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], though details remain unclear. The term "Shidori" is derived from "Shizuori" (倭文, meaning woven fabric). The deity {{ill|Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Shidori-be|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori-be|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, a weaving guild. This suggests that a community of Shidori-be people settled in the area and established the shrine. The shrine’s name is thought to have influenced the local place name "Kaminomiya" (Upper Shrine). It faces {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|火雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} (Shimonomiya, Lower Shrine) across the Tone River, though historical records indicate that the two were once connected before the river changed course in the medieval period''Gunma Prefecture Place Names,'' Sawa District, Tamamura Town, Shimonomiya Village. === Historical Records === The first mention of the shrine in historical documents is in the ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (859 CE), where it appears as "Ibumi Shrine" (委文神) and was granted the status of an official government shrine. That same year, it was elevated to the Rank of ''Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade'' (従五位下). In the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'' (927 CE), the shrine is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, confirming its importance in the [[:en:Nawa District, Gunma|Naba District]] of Kōzuke Province. In 1030, the shrine is recorded in the ''Jōgen 3 Exchange Records'' (上野国交替実録帳) as "Ibumi Myōjin Shrine" (委文明神社), holding the Rank of ''Senior Third Rank'' (正三位). The ''[[:en:Shintōshū|Jindōshū]]'' (compiled in the Nanboku-chō period) refers to the shrine as the 9th shrine of Kōzuke Province. === Edo Period and Beyond === During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the temple {{ill|Jigen-ji|ja|慈眼寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigen-ji|qq=}} became the shrine's caretaker, and in 1648, the Tokugawa Shogunate granted it an official landholding of 10 koku. In 1727, the shrine buildings were rebuilt but were later destroyed in 1866. During the Meiji Restoration, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha (Japan)|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Japan)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. In 1925, it was recognized as a recipient of imperial offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). == Shrine Structures == The main hall (''honden'') is built in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style with a copper roof and was rebuilt in 1880. File:Shidori-jinja (Isesaki) honden.JPG|Main Hall File:Shidori-jinja (Isesaki) haiden-2.JPG|Worship Hall == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties of Isesaki City === * '''Important Cultural Property''' ** Shidori Shrine’s Red Seal Certificates – A set of nine ''shuinjō'' (朱印状) issued since 1648, designated in 1992. * '''Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property''' ** ''Ta-asobi'' (田遊び) – A traditional rice-planting ritual performed on January 14, designated in 2007. == Related Shrines == * {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|火雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} – Located in Tamamura Town, historically connected as "Shimonomiya" (Lower Shrine). == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shidori-jinja (Isesaki)}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as Amanoorihime) }} }} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{citation needed|ja|静神社|version=101958783|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] hengaku.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Plaque with Shrine Name]] According to shrine tradition, Shidori Shrine was founded in the 3rd year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], though details remain unclear. The term "Shidori" is derived from "Shizuori" (倭文, meaning woven fabric). The deity {{ill|Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hazuchi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Shidori-be|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori-be|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, a weaving guild. This suggests that a community of Shidori-be people settled in the area and established the shrine. The shrine’s name is thought to have influenced the local place name "Kaminomiya" (Upper Shrine). It faces {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|火雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} (Shimonomiya, Lower Shrine) across the Tone River, though historical records indicate that the two were once connected before the river changed course in the medieval period''Gunma Prefecture Place Names,'' Sawa District, Tamamura Town, Shimonomiya Village. === Historical Records === The first mention of the shrine in historical documents is in the ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (859 CE), where it appears as "Ibumi Shrine" (委文神) and was granted the status of an official government shrine. That same year, it was elevated to the Rank of ''Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade'' (従五位下). In the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'' (927 CE), the shrine is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, confirming its importance in the [[:en:Nawa District, Gunma|Naba District]] of Kōzuke Province. In 1030, the shrine is recorded in the ''Jōgen 3 Exchange Records'' (上野国交替実録帳) as "Ibumi Myōjin Shrine" (委文明神社), holding the Rank of ''Senior Third Rank'' (正三位). The ''[[:en:Shintōshū|Jindōshū]]'' (compiled in the Nanboku-chō period) refers to the shrine as the 9th shrine of Kōzuke Province. === Edo Period and Beyond === During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the temple {{ill|Jigen-ji|ja|慈眼寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigen-ji|qq=}} became the shrine's caretaker, and in 1648, the Tokugawa Shogunate granted it an official landholding of 10 koku. In 1727, the shrine buildings were rebuilt but were later destroyed in 1866. During the Meiji Restoration, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha (Japan)|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Japan)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. In 1925, it was recognized as a recipient of imperial offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). == Shrine Structures == The main hall (''honden'') is built in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style with a copper roof and was rebuilt in 1880. File:Shidori-jinja (Isesaki) honden.JPG|Main Hall File:Shidori-jinja (Isesaki) haiden-2.JPG|Worship Hall == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties of Isesaki City === * '''Important Cultural Property''' ** Shidori Shrine’s Red Seal Certificates – A set of nine ''shuinjō'' (朱印状) issued since 1648, designated in 1992. * '''Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property''' ** ''Ta-asobi'' (田遊び) – A traditional rice-planting ritual performed on January 14, designated in 2007. == Related Shrines == * {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|火雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} – Located in Tamamura Town, historically connected as "Shimonomiya" (Lower Shrine). == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shidori-jinja (Isesaki)}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as Amanoorihime) }} }} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{citation needed|ja|静神社|version=101958783|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]]
